description: Controls how Claude Code workers select and rotate API providers for quota-aware concurrency. Secrets are never stored here — only provider identifiers, concurrency limits, and cooldown rules.

providers.label: Default Claude Code credential
providers.notes: Local Claude Code credential capacity for the current controlled self-cycle rehearsal. Secrets remain local; this value only declares scheduling capacity.

concurrency.notes: Global cap is the hard ceiling. Per-provider maxConcurrency is the soft cap. When all providers are exhausted or at capacity, new worker launches are blocked (fail-closed).

triggers.notes: HTTP 429 from the provider API. Provider is marked exhausted and cooldown starts.
Provider reports quota exhaustion (distinct from rate-limit 429). Longer cooldown because quota resets are slower.
Credential rejected. Provider is disabled until manual intervention. Never auto-recovers.

recovery.notes: After cooldown expires, the next selection attempt probes the provider before routing a real worker to it.

quotaExhaustion.notes: Distinguished from runtime/validation failures. Exhaustion is a resource constraint, not a code bug.

authFailure.notes: Credential is invalid or revoked. Requires manual fix.

runtimeFailure.notes: Transient provider-side errors. Does not mark provider exhausted.

providerAssignment.notes: Workers receive a provider id via environment variable. The actual secret (API key) is injected from local-only sources (credential manager, env vars, local config). The secret never flows through task JSON, issue bodies, or git.

telemetry.notes: Workers record which provider they used and the quota state at task start/end. Secrets are explicitly excluded from telemetry.

launchGateIntegration.notes: The launch gate reads provider-pool.json state before dispatching. If no provider is available (all exhausted or at capacity), the batch is blocked.
